title: schisandra henryi subsp. marginalis
intro: Schisandra henryi subsp. marginalis is a subspecies of the Schisandra henryi plant, a type of woody vine native to China, characterized by its unique combination of five distinct fruit flavors, known as the "five tastes": sweet, sour, bitter, salty, and umami. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and adaptogenic properties, which can help to enhance physical and mental resilience. The health benefits of Schisandra henryi subsp. marginalis include improved immune function, enhanced cognitive function, and reduced stress levels, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional Chinese medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, capsules, and extracts, which can be consumed for its medicinal properties.
